## How It's Calculated

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (financial, educational, legal), consult with a qualified professional. Values are updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What GPA do I need for a Chevening scholarship?
faq.a: Chevening typically requires a minimum GPA equivalent to an upper second-class degree (usually 3.0–3.5 on a 4.0 scale). Requirements vary by country and program.

faq.q: What's the minimum GPA for Fulbright?
faq.a: Fulbright generally requires a cumulative GPA of 3.0 or higher on a 4.0 scale, though specific requirements vary by country and program type.

faq.q: How is GPA calculated for scholarship applications?
faq.a: Most scholarships use your cumulative GPA from your official academic records. Some recalculate based on specific coursework. Always check the target scholarship's requirements.

faq.q: Does this calculator work for all countries?
faq.a: Yes. This calculator helps assess GPA requirements for major international scholarships applicable across countries and regions.
Yes. We update our data regularly with 2026 information. Always verify directly with the official scholarship provider for the most current requirements.

faq.q: Is this calculator really free with no sign-up?
faq.a: Yes. All our calculators are completely free with no registration, login, or email required.

faq.q: What if my GPA is below the minimum requirement?
faq.a: Many scholarships consider additional factors beyond GPA: work experience, test scores, essays, and recommendations. Don't hesitate to apply if your GPA is slightly below the stated minimum.

faq.q: How accurate are these calculator results?
faq.a: Results are guidelines only. We use standard, internationally-validated formulas. For critical decisions, contact the scholarship provider directly to confirm eligibility.
